API Reference¶

model — Neural Fields and Learnable Parameters¶

Neural fields, learnable parameters, and related models.

class kine.model.AmplitudeGains(lower: ~jax.Array | ~numpy.ndarray | ~numpy.bool_ | ~numpy.number | bool | int | float | complex, upper: ~jax.Array | ~numpy.ndarray | ~numpy.bool_ | ~numpy.number | bool | int | float | complex, nsites: int = 8, ntimes: int = 99, parent: ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module.Module] | ~typing.Type[~flax.core.scope.Scope] | ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module._Sentinel] | None = <flax.linen.module._Sentinel object>, name: str | None = None)¶

Bases: Module

Train a multi-dimensional parameter as amplitude gains.

Args:

lower: Gains values lower limit. upper: Gains values upper limit. nsite: Number of telescopes. ntimes: Number of time segments over which gains are computed.

clipping(x: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex, site: str) → Array¶

Clip gains within specified range.

Args:

x: Input array. site: Telescope codename.

Returns:

Clipped (or Sigmoided) input array.

lower: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex¶
name: str | None = None¶
nsites: int = 8¶
ntimes: int = 99¶
parent: Type[Module] | Type[Scope] | Type[_Sentinel] | None = None¶
scope: Scope | None = None¶
upper: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex¶
class kine.model.NeuralField(posenc_deg: tuple[int] = (0, 0, 0), outdim: int = 1, depth: int = 6, width: int = 256, activ: ~collections.abc.Callable[[...], ~typing.Any] = <function gelu>, outactiv: ~collections.abc.Callable[[...], ~typing.Any] = <PjitFunction of <function softplus>>, outshift: int = 10, do_bnorm: bool = True, skipat: int = 0, scaling_i: float = 1.0, scaling_ml: float = 1.0, parent: ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module.Module] | ~typing.Type[~flax.core.scope.Scope] | ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module._Sentinel] | None = <flax.linen.module._Sentinel object>, name: str | None = None)¶

Bases: Module

Neural Field implementation.

Predicts full pol. emission at space-time coordinates (x, y, t). Input coordinates are transformed through poisitional encoding and fed to an MLP. We use batch normalization and skip connections.

Args:

posenc_deg: Degrees of positional encoding outdim: Output layer dimension depth: Number of hidden layers width: Number of neurons in each hidden layer activ: Activation function for hidden layers outactiv: Output activation function outshift: Output activation function shift do_bnorm: If true, do batch normalization skipat: Skip connection layer scaling_i: Sigmoid scaling for Stokes I ([0, 1] -> [0, scaling_i]) scaling_ml: Sigmoid scaling for ml ([0, 1] -> [0, scaling_ml])

activ(approximate: bool = True) → Array¶

Gaussian error linear unit activation function.

If approximate=False, computes the element-wise function:

\[\mathrm{gelu}(x) = \frac{x}{2} \left(1 + \mathrm{erf} \left( \frac{x}{\sqrt{2}} \right) \right)\]

If approximate=True, uses the approximate formulation of GELU:

\[\mathrm{gelu}(x) = \frac{x}{2} \left(1 + \mathrm{tanh} \left( \sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi}} \left(x + 0.044715 x^3 \right) \right) \right)\]

For more information, see Gaussian Error Linear Units (GELUs), section 2.

Args:

x : input array approximate: whether to use the approximate or exact formulation.

depth: int = 6¶
do_bnorm: bool = True¶
name: str | None = None¶
outactiv() → Array¶

Softplus activation function.

Computes the element-wise function

\[\mathrm{softplus}(x) = \log(1 + e^x)\]
Args:

x : input array

outdim: int = 1¶
outshift: int = 10¶
parent: Type[Module] | Type[Scope] | Type[_Sentinel] | None = None¶
posenc_deg: tuple[int] = (0, 0, 0)¶
scaling_i: float = 1.0¶
scaling_ml: float = 1.0¶
scope: Scope | None = None¶
skipat: int = 0¶
width: int = 256¶
class kine.model.NeuralFieldPol(posenc_deg: tuple[int] = (0, 0, 0), outdim: int = 3, depth: int = 6, width: int = 256, activ: ~collections.abc.Callable[[...], ~typing.Any] = <function gelu>, outactiv: ~collections.abc.Callable[[...], ~typing.Any] = <PjitFunction of <function sigmoid>>, outshift: int = 10, do_bnorm: bool = True, skipat: int = 0, scaling_ml: float = 1.0, parent: ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module.Module] | ~typing.Type[~flax.core.scope.Scope] | ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module._Sentinel] | None = <flax.linen.module._Sentinel object>, name: str | None = None)¶

Bases: Module

Linear polarization Neural Field implementation.

Predicts lin. pol. emission at space-time coordinates (x, y, t). Input coordinates are transformed through poisitional encoding and fed to an MLP. We use batch normalization and skip connections.

Args:

posenc_deg: Degrees of positional encoding outdim: Output layer dimension depth: Number of hidden layers width: Number of neurons in each hidden layer activ: Activation function for hidden layers outactiv: Output activation function outshift: Output activation function shift do_bnorm: If true, do batch normalization skipat: Skip connection layer scaling_ml: Sigmoid scaling for ml ([0, 1] -> [0, scaling_ml])

activ(approximate: bool = True) → Array¶

Gaussian error linear unit activation function.

If approximate=False, computes the element-wise function:

\[\mathrm{gelu}(x) = \frac{x}{2} \left(1 + \mathrm{erf} \left( \frac{x}{\sqrt{2}} \right) \right)\]

If approximate=True, uses the approximate formulation of GELU:

\[\mathrm{gelu}(x) = \frac{x}{2} \left(1 + \mathrm{tanh} \left( \sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi}} \left(x + 0.044715 x^3 \right) \right) \right)\]

For more information, see Gaussian Error Linear Units (GELUs), section 2.

Args:

x : input array approximate: whether to use the approximate or exact formulation.

depth: int = 6¶
do_bnorm: bool = True¶
name: str | None = None¶
outactiv() → Array¶

Sigmoid activation function.

Computes the element-wise function:

\[\mathrm{sigmoid}(x) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-x}}\]
Args:

x : input array

Returns:

An array.

See also:

log_sigmoid()

outdim: int = 3¶
outshift: int = 10¶
parent: Type[Module] | Type[Scope] | Type[_Sentinel] | None = None¶
posenc_deg: tuple[int] = (0, 0, 0)¶
scaling_ml: float = 1.0¶
scope: Scope | None = None¶
skipat: int = 0¶
width: int = 256¶
class kine.model.PhaseGains(nsites: int = 8, ntimes: int = 99, parent: ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module.Module] | ~typing.Type[~flax.core.scope.Scope] | ~typing.Type[~flax.linen.module._Sentinel] | None = <flax.linen.module._Sentinel object>, name: str | None = None)¶

Bases: Module

Train a multi-dimensional parameter as phase gains.

Args:

nsite: Number of telescopes. ntimes: Number of time segments over which gains are computed.

clipping(x: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex) → Array¶

Clip gains within specified range.

Args:

x: Input array.

Returns:

Clipped input array.

name: str | None = None¶
nsites: int = 8¶
ntimes: int = 99¶
parent: Type[Module] | Type[Scope] | Type[_Sentinel] | None = None¶
scope: Scope | None = None¶
kine.model.posenc(x: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex, degs: tuple[int]) → Array¶

Positional encoding.

Concatenate x with a positional encoding of x with degree deg. Instead of computing [sin(x), cos(x)], we use the trig. identity cos(x) = sin(x + pi/2) and do one vectorized call to sin([x, x+pi/2]).

Args:

x: Variable to be encoded. Note that x should be in [-pi, pi]. deg: The degree of the encoding.

Returns:

Encoded variables.

kine.model.sharpgelu(x: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex, s: float = 3.0) → Array¶

Custom GELU activation function.

Computes a sharper variant of the (approximate) Gaussian Error Linear Unit (GELU) activation function.

Args:

x: input array. s: sharpness factor.

Returns:

The sharpGELU activation function.

obsdata — Data Handling¶

trainer — Training and Loss Functions¶

Trainer object. A compilation of useful methods for training.

kine.trainer.NPIX: int = 0¶

Global variable required for NUFFT computations.

This is currently the best way I’ve found for passing this value to a @jax.jit function. NPIX should be updated from main. See examples.

class kine.trainer.Trainer(step: int, apply_fn: Callable, params: FrozenDict[str, Any], tx: GradientTransformation, opt_state: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | Iterable[ArrayTree] | Mapping[Any, ArrayTree], batch_stats: Any | None = None)¶

Bases: TrainState

Compilation of useful functions for training.

Adds batch normalization to flax.training.train_state.TrainState. In addition, all functions related to training, like loss functions, are included here as (mostly private) static methods for consistency.

Args:

batch_stats: Batch normalization statistics

batch_stats: Any | None = None¶
replace(**updates)¶

“Returns a new object replacing the specified fields with new values.

static train_step(kwargs: OrderedDict) → list[Array]¶

Training step.

Args:
kwargs: Training states and other variables required

for loss computations. Input as an OrderedDict since @jax.jit can change the order in which kwargs are passed.

Returns:

Total loss, loss dictionary, sampled video, and training states

video — Video Creation and Visualization¶

utils — Utilities¶

Utils, helper functions, etc.

class kine.utils.HyperParams(params: dict)¶

Bases: object

Create a class object to store hyperparameters.

class kine.utils.Schedule(lr_i: float, lr_f: float, niter: int)¶

Bases: object

Create a learning rate schedule.

Attributes:

lr_i: Initial learning rate. lr_f: Final learning rate. niter: Number of training iterations.

Todo:

  • Add more custom schedules

exponential(count: int) → Array¶

Exponential learning rate schedule.

Args:

count: Current iteration.

Returns:

Learning rate value at given iteration.

kine.utils.batchify(batch: list | Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex, *args) → list[Array] | Array¶

Batch arrays.

Args:

batch: Sequence of indices for batching. *args: Arrays to batch.

Returns:

Batched arrays.

kine.utils.get_grid(nx: int, ny: int, nt: int | None = None, times: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex | None = None, tdil: float = 10) → Array¶

Generate grid of space-time coordinates.

The network is trained to predict the emission at locations (x, y, t) given by the initial grid of coordinates, but will learn a smooth interpolation between them that can be later sampled by passing a different (finer and time-homogeneous) grid of coordinates.

Args:

nx: Number of spatial locations in Right Ascension. ny: Number of spatial locations in Declination. nt: Number of time locations. times: Array of (irregular) time locations. tdil: Time dilation factor.

Returns:

Grid of space-time coordinates.

kine.utils.get_static_flux(found_flux: float, min_lcurve: float, min_flux_offset: float = 0.1) → float¶

Determine static flux density.

Args:

found_flux: Flux density found through regularization. min_lcurve: Light-curve minimum value. min_flux_offset: Offset from light-curve minimum.

kine.utils.get_times_multiepoch(inpaths: str, ymd: bool = False) → Array¶

Extract observation times from multiepoch file paths.

Args:

inpaths: ymd: If True, return times in YYYY-MM-DD format. If False,

return times in mjd format (required for training).

Returns:

Array of dates.

kine.utils.init_worker(fn: Callable, *args) → None¶

Asynchronous worker for CPU plotting.

Args:

fn: Asynchronous plotting function. *args: Queue object.

kine.utils.list_to_jaxarr(*args) → Array | list[Array]¶

Convert a list of arguments to JAX arrays.

kine.utils.map_val_to_ind(arr1: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex, arr2: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex) → Array¶

Map arr2 values to indices in arr1.

kine.utils.no_print()¶

Suppress stdout within the context.

kine.utils.pad(arr: list[Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex]) → Array¶

Pad an inhomogeneous list to create an array.

Args:

arr: Inhomogeneous array list.

Returns:

Concatenated array with homogeneous shape.

kine.utils.stack_and_pad(arr: list[Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex]) → Array¶

Stack and pad an inhomogeneous list to create an array.

Args:

arr: Inhomogeneous array list.

Returns:

Concatenated array with homogeneous shape.

kine.utils.to_complex(arr: Array | ndarray | bool_ | number | bool | int | float | complex) → Array¶

Turn real-valued JAX array to complex type.
